ticket.subject: Shipping Address Changes
ticket.description:
Hello guys!

 

I hope you are doing well. I am interested if you are able to make an update to the plugin to automatically change the shipping address in the plugin when it is updated on the Order Details page in Woocommerce.

 

Right now If I change the Shipping address from Address A to Address B on the order, the shipping plugin keeps Address A as the shipping address. We then have to manually change the shipping address in the shipping plugin so the order goes to the correct updated address B.

Is there any way you can automatically update the new shipping address in the Shipping Plugin when changes are saved in the Order Details Page?

 

Here’s a video explaining a bit more: https://www.loom.com/share/c94f5388d583456c98bedb3dbddf1fb5

 

Thanks so much!

 

Josef Bosnyak

Sales Executive

RaceTech Titanium

 

(618) 307-5637

JosefB@RaceTechTitanium.com

RaceTechTitanium.com (https://racetechtitanium.com/)

805 Biltmore Blvd. South Roxana, IL 62087

comments.body:
I discussed this case with Vimal.

Veena will further review this with the production team and confirm the feasibility.

**Case:** Auto Order Update Sync Option

**Details:**
A $49 Premium plan WSS customer has requested an automatic order update sync feature. They would like any updates made to the WooCommerce order (such as changes to address or items) to automatically reflect in WSS, so they can process orders with the updated details.

Currently, there is no order update sync feature. As a result, the customer must manually cross-check each order before generating labels. This process is time-consuming, and in several instances, order updates were missed, leading to deliveries being sent to incorrect addresses or with incorrect details.

**Store Details:**
Store URL: https://racetechtitanium.com
Account ID: 50f8db8e-31b9-4cf7-a40f-2caa9a14be89
Active From: 2023-12-07
Plan: $49 Premium Plan

comments.body:
Hello Josef,

Thank you for your reply.

The order updates should reflect within a short time (usually within a minute). As soon as you update the order in WooCommerce and click **Update/Save**, the changes should automatically sync with the plugin.
Could you please share the results from your testing? Specifically:
- Is the updated address reflecting automatically in the plugin?
- Did the update happen within a minute, or how long did it take?


If possible, could you also share a **short video recording** of the testing? This would help us monitor the steps being followed and check if any changes or improvements are needed.

Regarding the **“Add Flat Rate”** shipping option, I would like to clarify that when a customer provides an incorrect address and live shipping rates fail, the flat rate you configured will be displayed as the shipping cost, using the **rule name as the shipping option**.

To avoid this behavior, we recommend using the **“Adjust Shipment Price”** option instead of **“Add Flat Rate.”** The **Adjust Shipment Price** option allows you to apply a percentage-based adjustment to the actual calculated shipping rates. If the carrier-calculated rates fail, the percentage adjustment will also fail, allowing the **fallback shipping rate from WooCommerce settings** to be displayed instead.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hey Manohar!

Here is the Loom video you requested! https://www.loom.com/share/095b5c4698cd4ab3bcb1f3f5390142ba

The plugin seems to be updating properly now. Yesterday it would take anywhere from 5-30 minutes to update during my testing. I have found that if an order is changed from cancelled to processing and information is updated, it does not update in the plugin but this really isn’t a big concern at all. I was just using test orders to test the functionality of the plugin and found that small issue.



Regarding FLAT_RATE

We have been using the plugin for a couple years now and the fallback rate has always worked. We have always used the “Add Flat Rate” option with USPS because we don’t want to ship through them unless absolutely necessary but also don’t want to penalize customers too much if they absolutely have to ship via USPS. Adding a flat fee of $10-11 to the shipping cost for USPS has been a good middle ground rather than a percentage, especially for international orders. Up until you sent me the email yesterday letting me know updates were made to the plugin, the Fallback Shipping Rate has worked just fine. We have never encountered the FLAT_RATE option until the 27th of this month.



Here are all of the Fallback Shipping Rate Orders: https://racetechtitanium.com/wp-admin/admin.php?page=wc-orders&status=all&s=shipping+rate&search-filter=all&action=-1&m=0&_shop_order_shipping_provider&_created_via&_customer_user&paged=1&action2=-1

Here are the 3 FLAT_RATE orders: https://racetechtitanium.com/wp-admin/admin.php?page=wc-orders&paged=1&status=all&s=FLAT_RATE&search-filter=all&action=-1&m=0&_shop_order_shipping_provider&_created_via&_customer_user&action2=-1

Thanks so much!

comments.body:
Hello Josef,

Thank you for your patience while we review this case internally.

Regarding **Case 1: Order updates not syncing after a canceled order is moved to processing**, may we know why you are looking to update the canceled order?
We would like to understand your workflow better, as per our understanding, once an order is canceled, no further updates are usually required. Please share more details on your use case so we can review this further from our end.

Regarding **Case 2: Flat rate showing as shipping cost when the shipping address is incorrect**, are you using any other plugins to manage shipping rates at checkout?
We would like to review this case from our end. To investigate further, could you please provide temporary admin access along with the login URL?

Regarding **Case 3: USPS rates failure when insurance is added**, you have connected your USPS account using the **Contract** rate type, so the plugin is attempting to fetch contract services and rates. However, when insurance is enabled, USPS is not returning any contract services and is instead returning services under **Commercial** rates.

This appears to indicate that your contract rates may not be available when insurance is enabled. We recommend reviewing this case directly with the USPS team. You may share the log files you provided with us for their further review. Please let me know if you need any additional details from our end.

If you would like to use commercial rates with insurance, you may need to connect the USPS account twice and configure rules to fetch both contract and commercial rates. In this setup, when contract rates are unavailable, the commercial rates can be displayed instead.

Please let us know how it goes or if you need any further assistance.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

I look forward to hearing from you.

Hello Josef,

Thank you for flagging this. Since UPS was working correctly before and has now stopped displaying rates entirely, the most likely cause is that the UPS OAuth connection has expired or been interrupted — this can happen independently of toggling the rates on or off.

Here are the steps to check:

1. Go to your WSS app settings and navigate to Settings > Carriers > UPS OAuth. Verify the connection status shows as active (green). If it shows as disconnected or expired, click to reconnect and re-authorize the UPS connection.
2. Once reconnected, go to the Rate Log and check for any specific error messages tied to UPS requests — this will tell us exactly what UPS is returning.
3. Also confirm that your UPS automation rules still have the correct services selected and the right shipping zones assigned. Occasionally a settings save in another area can affect automation rule configurations.

If the OAuth connection looks fine and the automation rules appear correct, please share the Rate Log ID for a failing UPS request (you can find this under the Rates tab in the app) and we will dig in directly. Alternatively, since you have offered access, our team can log in using the previously provided credentials to investigate and resolve this for you.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive
